steps.message:
[SYSTEM]
You are a careful and methodical reasoner.
Each turn, you MUST respond with ONLY a JSON block in this exact format:
```json
{
  "analysis": "<your observations and reasoning this turn>",
  "plan": "<what you intend to examine or verify next>",
  "answers": null
}
```
When you have verified all answers and are fully confident, set `answers` to a list of strings (one letter per question, in question order), for example:
```json
{
  "analysis": "<final verification notes>",
  "plan": "done",
  "answers": ["B", "A", "C"]
}
```
Do NOT set `answers` until you have genuinely cross-checked every question. Take as many turns as you need.

[USER]
You are solving a set of orthographic projection questions.

Each question is presented as a single composite image in standard PAT format:
- Left panel:  top view, front view, and end view, separated by orthographic reference lines. One of them has been replaced by a question mark. 
- Right panel: four candidate views for the question mark labelled A, B, C, D,

Select the one view that is fully consistent with the two other views.

Respond each turn with a JSON block as instructed. When you are confident in all your answers, set the "answers" field to a list of letters (one per question, in order).

There are 15 question(s) in total. The composite question image(s) follow.

```json
{
  "analysis": "Let me analyze each question systematically by examining the given views and determining which candidate matches.

Q1: Top view is missing. Front view shows a lamp-like shape with a trapezoidal top (wider at top, narrower at bottom) with a rectangular notch at bottom left and dashed lines showing hidden features (a square). End view is similar but with the notch on a different side. Looking at the candidates, the top view should show concentric circles (round body) with a square feature. Option A shows concentric circles with a square in the center and a stem below. This seems to match the circular/round lamp shape seen from above.

Q2: Front view is missing. Top view shows a square with diagonal lines and a small square and circle. End view shows a trapezoidal shape with vertical dashed lines and rectangular features. Option A shows a front view with appropriate features.

Q3: End view is missing. Top view shows an L-shaped or rectangular shape with a diagonal line. Front view shows a stepped shape with dashed lines. Looking at the options, B appears to match - showing a shape consistent with the depth and height information.

Q4: Top view is missing. Front view shows rectangular blocks with dashed lines. End view shows similar rectangular features. Options A and B show oval/elliptical shapes which suggest a curved surface. Option B seems more consistent.

Q5: End view is missing. Top view shows a tilted square/diamond shape with dashed lines on a stem/base. Front view shows a trapezoidal shape with multiple dashed lines. Option C seems to match.

Q6: Top view is missing. Front view shows a flat base with triangular and rectangular protrusions. End view is similar. Option C shows a rectangular top view with inner rectangles matching the features.

Q7: Front view is missing. Top view shows a rectangle with a circle. End view shows a triangular/wedge shape with dashed center line. Option A shows a rectangular front view with dashed lines consistent with the hole.

Q8: End view is missing. Top view shows complex interlocking shapes. Front view shows rectangular shape with multiple U-shaped notches. Option B seems to match the end view profile.

Q9: End view is missing. Top view shows a long narrow shape with features. Front view shows a rectangular shape with a small stepped feature on the left. Options show long horizontal views.

Q10: Top view is missing. Front view shows a trapezoidal shape (wider at bottom) with a small protrusion at top and a rectangular notch. End view shows similar trapezoidal shape with two vertical slots. Option B seems to match.

Q11: Top view is missing. Front and end views show flat horizontal shapes with small rectangular features. The top view should show the pac-man/crescent moon shape. Option D seems most consistent.

Q12: Top view is missing. Front view shows stepped blocks. End view shows similar stepped shape with dashed lines. Option A shows a circle with inner features.

Q13: End view is missing. Top view shows a circle with a square and cross-hatch pattern plus a protruding rectangular feature. Front view shows a rectangular base with a pointed peak/spike. Option A seems to match.

Q14: Front view is missing. Top view shows a rectangular shape with an L-shaped cutout. End view shows a stepped shape. Option C seems consistent.

Q15: Top view is missing. Front view shows a boat/vessel shape with a vertical post. End view shows a triangular shape. Option B seems to match with a circular shape and features.",
  "plan": "Let me re-examine each question more carefully before finalizing answers.",
  "answers": null
}
```

```json
{
  "analysis": "Let me re-examine each question more carefully.

Q1: The front and end views both show a lamp-like shape - trapezoidal body wider at top, narrow stem, small base. The front view has a dashed rectangle at top and a small notch at bottom-left. The end view has a dashed rectangle at top and a thin stem at bottom. Looking from above, the lamp shade would appear circular (concentric circles for the round shade), with a square/rectangular feature in the center (the stem/support). The stem extends downward in both views, so in top view it would show as a small rectangle below the circles. Option A shows concentric circles with a square in center and a small stem - this matches. Option D is smaller. I'll go with A.

Q2: Looking at the top view - it's a square with diagonal lines forming an X pattern, a small square near center-right, and a circle at bottom-left. The end view shows a trapezoidal body with vertical features. The front view should be a mirror/related projection. The circle in top view at bottom-left means from the front, it would appear as a circle or hidden feature on the left side. Option A shows appropriate rectangular/trapezoidal shape with dashed vertical lines. Option C has more detail. Let me think... The front view should show the circle as visible since it's at the front edge. Actually option A seems right.

Q3: Top view has a rectangular shape divided by a diagonal line, with a smaller rectangle on the right side. Front view shows a stepped shape - tall left portion and shorter right portion with a notch. The end view (looking from the right side) should show the depth profile. The diagonal in top view means there's a sloped surface. Option B shows a shape that matches - it has a step consistent with the front view height and the diagonal appears as a slope.

Q4: Front view shows rectangular blocks with various heights and dashed lines. End view shows similar rectangular features with dashed lines. The object appears to have an elliptical/oval component based on options A and B. Option A shows the oval tilted one way, B shows it tilted the other way. Looking at the front and end views more carefully - the front view has features offset to the left, and the end view has features more centered. Option B seems more consistent.

Q5: Top view shows a diamond/tilted square shape on a cross-shaped base. Front view shows a trapezoidal cup-like shape with many dashed lines and a small base. The end view should show a similar profile. Option C shows a trapezoidal shape with a diagonal line (representing the tilted top) and appropriate base features.

Q6: Front view shows a wide flat base with a triangular protrusion on the left, a rectangular block in the middle, and small features. End view shows similar features with dashed vertical lines. The top view should show the overall rectangular footprint with internal features. Option C shows a rectangle with inner rectangles matching the block features.

Q7: Top view is a rectangle with a circle (hole) in the upper portion. End view shows a right triangle shape (wedge) with dashed lines. The front view should show a rectangular shape (same width as top view, same height as end view) with the hole shown as hidden lines. Option A shows a tall rectangle with dashed vertical lines representing the hole, which seems right.

Q8: Top view shows a complex shape with interlocking T-shaped features. Front view shows a rectangle with multiple U-shaped notches at the bottom. The end view should show the depth profile. Option B shows a simple rectangular shape with a small rectangular feature, which seems most consistent.

Q9: Top view shows a long vertical strip with various protrusions and features. Front view shows a rectangular shape with a small stepped feature. The end view should be a long horizontal shape. Option A seems most consistent.

Q10: Front view shows a trapezoidal shape (wider at bottom) with a small bump at top and a rectangular feature at bottom-left with a dashed line. End view shows a trapezoidal shape with two vertical slots at the bottom. The top view should show the overall footprint. Option B shows a rectangular shape with the bump feature and horizontal extensions, which seems right.

Q11: Front and end views show very flat/thin horizontal shapes with small rectangular protrusions. The top view is a large pac-man/crescent shape. Looking at the options - they all show similar crescent shapes but differ in internal features. The front view shows small rectangular bumps, and the end view shows similar features. Option D shows a crescent with appropriate dashed lines and features.

Q12: Front view shows a stepped block structure (tall center, medium sides, with a notch). End view shows similar stepped structure with dashed diagonal lines. The top view should be circular (based on all options showing circles). Option A shows a circle with a small circle at top and rectangular features below.

Q13: Top view shows a circle with a square and crosshatch and a protruding rectangle to the right. Front view shows a rectangular base with a pointed spike/peak on top. The end view should show the side profile. Option B seems right - showing a rectangular base with the spike and appropriate hidden lines.

Q14: Front view is missing. Top view shows a rectangular shape with internal L-shaped cutouts and dashed lines. End view shows a stepped shape with dashed diagonal lines and small rectangles. Option C shows a front view that seems most consistent.

Q15: Top view is missing. Front view shows a boat/bowl shape with a tall vertical mast/post and internal dashed features. End view shows a triangular profile with dashed lines. The top view should be circular (boat is bowl-shaped from above). Option B shows a circle with appropriate features.",
  "plan": "Let me do one more careful pass through each question to verify my answers.",
  "answers": null
}
```

```json
{
  "analysis": "Final careful review of each question:

Q1: Front and end views show a lamp shape. Top view should show circular concentric shapes (round lamp shade) with a square/rectangular center feature and stem. Option A has the right size circles and square, matching the proportions. Answer: A

Q2: Top view has diagonal lines, a small square, and a circle. End view shows a complex trapezoidal shape. The front view needs to match both. Looking at the asymmetry - the circle is on the lower-left of the top view (which corresponds to the front-left in the front view). Option A shows a shape that could work. Actually looking more carefully, option D has some angled features that might match the diagonals better. I'll go with A.

Q3: Top view is rectangular with a diagonal dividing it, and a smaller rectangle jutting out on the right. Front view has a stepped profile. The end view (right side view) needs to show depth from the right. The diagonal in top view creates a slope visible from the end. Option B shows the right step profile without the diagonal complexity. Actually, looking again at option A - it has a dashed vertical line which could represent the step change in depth. Option B has a similar shape but with the dashed line in a slightly different position. I think B is correct.

Q4: Front and end views suggest blocks with an elliptical body. Option B shows the ellipse oriented correctly. Answer: B

Q5: The tilted square on a stand seen from the front creates a trapezoidal shape. The end view should show a similar but depth-wise profile. Option C shows a trapezoidal shape with diagonal and vertical dashed lines. Answer: C

Q6: The front view has triangular wedge shapes on the sides and rectangular blocks. The top view should reflect this layout. Option C has rectangles matching the block positions. Answer: C

Q7: Rectangle with hole from top, wedge from side. Front should show rectangle with hidden hole lines. Option A shows a tall rectangle with vertical dashed lines for the hole. Answer: A

Q8: Complex top view and front view with notches. End view should be simple profile. Option B shows a stepped rectangular profile that matches. Answer: B

Q9: Long narrow top view with features, simple front view. End view should be long and flat. Option A seems correct. Answer: A

Q10: Trapezoidal front and end views with different features. Top view shows the footprint. Option B has the right proportions. Answer: B

Q11: Very thin front and end views with small features. Top view is a crescent/pac-man shape. Option D has appropriate features matching the front and end views. Answer: D

Q12: Stepped front and end views. Top view should be circular with features. Option A has a small circle (dashed) and rectangle features. Answer: A

Q13: Circle with features in top view, spiked front view. End view should match. Option B has the right proportions. Answer: B

Q14: L-shaped top view, complex end view. Front view should match both. Option C appears most consistent. Answer: C

Q15: Bowl-shaped front view with mast, triangular end view. Top view should be circular. Option B appears correct. Answer: B",
  "plan": "Let me reconsider a few uncertain answers before finalizing.

Q2: I need to look more carefully. The top view shows diagonal lines going from corners to a central square, with a circle at bottom-left. The end view is complex. Front view options A-D all show trapezoidal shapes but differ in hidden lines and surface details. I'll stick with A.

Q3: Reconsidering - the end view should show what the object looks like from the right side. The top view diagonal line suggests a sloped surface. Looking at options again: A has a notch and dashed line, B is simpler with a step. I think B is correct.

Q11: Looking more carefully at the front and end views - they're very similar flat shapes with small rectangular protrusions. Options B, C, D all look similar. The key difference is the circles/holes and dashed lines. Option D seems right with its dashed lines matching the hidden features.

I'm fairly confident in most answers. Let me finalize.",
  "answers": null
}
```

```json
{
  "analysis": "Let me do one final careful pass:

Q1: TOP VIEW needed. Front/end views show a lamp shape (trapezoid body, stem, base). From above, the round shade appears as concentric circles, with a square visible inside (the top of the shade opening). The stem appears below. Answer: A (large concentric circles with centered square and stem)

Q2: FRONT VIEW needed. Top view shows a square with diagonals converging to center, small square at center-right, circle at bottom-left. End view shows trapezoidal shape with rectangular protrusions. The front view should show the depth features from the front. The circle in bottom-left of top view is near the front. Answer: A

Q3: END VIEW needed. Top view: rectangle with diagonal line from upper-left to lower-right, with an extension on the right side. Front view: stepped shape (taller left, shorter right with notch). End view from right should show the depth profile. The diagonal creates a sloping surface. Answer: B (step shape matching front view height)

Q4: TOP VIEW needed. Front view shows rectangular blocks. End view shows rectangular blocks with dashed features. The presence of oval shapes in options A and B suggests the object has a curved/elliptical body. Answer: B (oval shape oriented correctly with rectangular features)

Q5: END VIEW needed. Complex object with tilted cube on stem and base. Answer: C

Q6: TOP VIEW needed. Front view has flat base, triangular wedges, rectangular block. End view similar. Top view should show rectangular plan with features. Answer: C

Q7: FRONT VIEW needed. Top: rectangle with circle. End: right triangle/wedge. Front should be rectangular with circle shown as hidden dashed lines. Answer: A

Q8: END VIEW needed. Complex top and front views. Answer: B

Q9: END VIEW needed. Long narrow object. Answer: A

Q10: TOP VIEW needed. Trapezoidal front/end views. Answer: B

Q11: TOP VIEW needed. Thin flat object with features. Answer: D

Q12: TOP VIEW needed. Stepped blocks. Answer: A

Q13: END VIEW needed. Circular top view with spike in front view. Answer: B

Q14: FRONT VIEW needed. Answer: C

Q15: TOP VIEW needed. Bowl with mast. Answer: B",
  "plan": "I'll finalize my answers now.",
  "answers": ["A", "A", "B", "B", "C", "C", "A", "B", "A", "B", "D", "A", "B", "C", "B"]
}
```
Continue your analysis. Respond with a JSON block containing your `analysis`, `plan`, and `answers` (null if not yet ready). There are 15 question(s) total.

```json
{
  "analysis": "Let me re-examine each question more carefully, especially the ones I'm less confident about.

Q1: TOP VIEW needed. Front view: trapezoid wider at top, narrow stem, small rectangular base on left side. Has dashed square near top. End view: similar trapezoid but stem is centered at bottom, appears narrower. Both views show a rounded/circular object (lamp shade shape). From top, concentric circles with inner features. Option A: large outer circle, dashed circle, solid square in center, stem below. Option D: smaller circles with dashed square. The front view shows the dashed square near top center - this should appear as a square in the top view. Option A has a solid square centered, Option D has a dashed square. Since the square is visible from top, it should be solid. Answer: A

Q2: FRONT VIEW needed. Top view: large square with diagonal lines from corners toward center, small square near center-right, circle at bottom-left. End view: trapezoidal shape wider at top with vertical dashed lines and rectangular protrusions at bottom. The front view should match the width of the top view and height of the end view. The circle in the top view at bottom-left is near the front face. Looking at options: A shows trapezoidal shape with rectangular protrusion at top-left and dashed vertical lines. B shows similar but with dashed diagonal lines instead. The diagonals in the top view represent sloping surfaces that would appear as diagonal hidden lines in the front view. But wait - the end view doesn't show diagonals, it shows vertical dashed lines. So the diagonals in top view create slopes visible from front but not from end... Actually, I think option A is correct - it matches the end view's height and shows appropriate features.

Q3: END VIEW needed. Top view: rectangular shape with a diagonal line from upper-left area to lower-right, and a narrower rectangle extending to the right. Front view: stepped shape - tall rectangle on left, shorter section on right with a notch cut from bottom-right. The end view from the right should show the depth (left-right in top view becomes depth in end view). The diagonal in top view means there's a slope. Looking at options: A shows a rectangle with a step and dashed vertical line. B shows a simpler shape with a step and dashed vertical line. Actually, looking more carefully at the front view, the bottom has a stepped notch. From the end (right side), we'd see the depth profile. Option B seems like a simple step shape which could be correct.

Q4: TOP VIEW needed. Front view: shows rectangular blocks of different heights with vertical dashed lines. There appear to be tall outer blocks and shorter middle sections. End view: similar rectangular profile with dashed lines. Options A and B show elliptical shapes - suggesting the object is partially cylindrical/elliptical. Options C and D show T-shaped or cross-shaped top views. Looking at front view more carefully - there are tall rectangular blocks on the sides with a lower middle section, and small rectangular features at the bottom. The end view shows similar features. An elliptical top view (A or B) suggests the outer boundary is curved. Option A has the ellipse on the left with small rectangles on the right. Option B has the ellipse on the left with small rectangles more centered. I think B is more consistent.

Q5: END VIEW needed. Top view shows a tilted square/diamond shape at top with dashed lines, a cross-shaped stem in middle, and appears to have a base. Front view shows a trapezoidal cup shape with many dashed/dotted lines showing internal features, small rectangles at bottom. The end view should show the side profile. Option A shows a small rectangle and a trapezoidal shape with diagonal dotted line. Option C shows a trapezoidal shape with a large diagonal and dashed vertical lines. Looking at the top view, the diamond shape would create diagonal profiles from the side. Answer: C seems right.

Q6: TOP VIEW needed. Front view: flat base plate with triangular wedge on left side, rectangular block on right side, and smaller rectangular features between them. End view: similar flat base with triangular wedge on right and rectangular features with dashed vertical lines. From top, we'd see the rectangular base plate with the triangular and rectangular features on it. Option A: U-shape. Option B: rectangle with circle and rectangle inside. Option C: rectangle with two inner rectangles (matching block positions). Option D: rectangle with dotted lines and stepped features. The triangles in front/end views suggest triangular prisms which from top would appear as rectangles (the base of the triangle). Option C shows inner rectangles which could represent the blocks. Answer: C

Q7: FRONT VIEW needed. Top view: rectangle with a circle in upper portion. End view: right triangle (wedge shape) with dashed center line, wider at bottom, tapering to right at top. The front view should show the width from the top view and the height from the end view. The wedge profile from the end means the front view would show a rectangle (full height on the left side matching the tall part of the wedge). The circle would appear as hidden dashed lines since it's a through-hole. Option A: tall rectangle with two vertical dashed lines (representing the hole). Option D: shorter rectangle with dashed lines. The height should match the end view's maximum height. Answer: A

Q8: END VIEW needed. Top view shows interlocking/complex shape with T-shapes and rectangles. Front view shows a large rectangle with multiple U-shaped notches at the bottom (like castle battlements). The end view should show the side profile - the depth from top view and height from front view. Option A shows a stepped shape with dashed lines. Option B shows a simpler rectangular profile with a small rectangle. The front view is tall with notches at bottom; from the side, notches wouldn't be visible (they'd be hidden). Option B seems simpler and more appropriate. Answer: B

Q9: END VIEW needed. Top view: long vertical strip with various protrusions (small rectangles sticking out to sides) and dotted features. Front view: rectangular shape with small stepped features on the left side, showing dotted/dashed squares. The end view should show the object from the right side - it would be long horizontally (matching the top view's vertical extent) and have the height from the front view. Option A shows a long flat shape with small features. Option B is similar but with slightly different features. The front view is short/squat with features on the left. From the end, the length of the top view becomes the width. Answer: A

Q10: TOP VIEW needed. Front view: trapezoidal shape wider at bottom, small bump on top center, rectangular notch at bottom-left with dashed line connecting. End view: trapezoidal shape wider at bottom with two vertical rectangular slots at bottom center. The top view should show the plan. Option A: rectangle with smaller dashed rectangles inside and extensions on sides. Option B: rectangle with solid smaller rectangle and extensions. The trapezoidal shape from front and end means the top view would show nested rectangles (wider base, narrower top). Option B shows a cleaner plan view. Answer: B

Q11: TOP VIEW needed. Front view: very flat/thin horizontal bar with small rectangular protrusions on each end. End view: similar thin bar with small rectangular protrusions. The top view is a large shape (pac-man/crescent). Looking at options: A has a crescent with a dashed circle and vertical bar at top. B has crescent with a solid circle and dashed rectangles at bottom. C has crescent with solid circles and dashed rectangles. D has crescent with a solid circle and dashed rectangles plus a vertical bar at top. The front view shows a thin vertical bar on top - this would appear in top view as a rectangle above the crescent. Options A and D have this vertical bar. Looking at the front view more carefully - there are small rectangular bumps on the ends of the flat bar. Option D shows the vertical bar and appropriate features. Answer: D

Q12: TOP VIEW needed. Front view: stepped blocks - wider base, medium width middle, narrow tall center, with a small notch. End view: similar stepped blocks with dashed diagonal lines. Top view should be circular (all options show circles). Option A: circle with dashed small circle at top and rectangular features. Option B: circle with angular features and solid shapes inside. Option C: circle with smaller circle and rectangular bar inside. Option D: circle with small features. The stepped blocks from front/end with circular top suggest a cylindrical object with rectangular cutouts/steps. Answer: A

Q13: END VIEW needed. Top view: circle on left with a square and cross-hatched pattern inside, rectangular protrusion extending to the right. Front view: rectangular base with narrow pointed spike/peak rising from the center-right, with dashed vertical lines. The end view from right side should show: the height profile including the spike, and the depth from the top view. The circular part in top view means from the end, we'd see a curved outline. Option A: rectangular base with spike, some dashed lines. Option B: similar but slightly different proportions. Looking at the top view - the rectangular protrusion extends to the right, so from the right end view, this protrusion would be visible in front. The circular part would be behind it. Answer: B

Q14: FRONT VIEW needed. Top view: rectangular shape with an L-shaped or hook-shaped cutout, dashed lines showing hidden features. End view: stepped shape with rectangular protrusions and dashed diagonal/cross lines. The front view should match the top view's width and end view's height. Option A: stepped shape with dashed vertical lines and small rectangle at right. Option C: stepped shape with dashed vertical lines and small rectangle at right. A and C look very similar. The difference might be in the position of features. Looking carefully, option C seems to have the hidden lines positioned correctly. Answer: C

Q15: TOP VIEW needed. Front view: bowl/boat shape (V-shaped bottom) with a tall vertical mast/post in the center, some dashed features showing internal structure, small rectangle at bottom. End view: triangular shape (like a sail or wedge) with dashed internal lines and a small feature at bottom. From top, the bowl would appear as an elongated shape (oval or circular depending on the bowl shape), and the mast as a rectangle. The triangular end view suggests the shape narrows. Options A and B show circles with features. Option A has a dashed circle inside and features. Option B has a smaller solid circle. The bowl shape from top would be circular/oval. Answer: B",
  "plan": "I'm fairly confident in my answers now. Let me compile the final list.",
  "answers": ["A", "A", "B", "B", "C", "C", "A", "B", "A", "B", "D", "A", "B", "C", "B"]
}
```
